---
title: "Week 5 — Association"
toc: true
---

> **Status:** placeholder. Real content will be developed during the
> course build.

## Big question

Are two variables related?

## Day-by-day

### Monday — concept

Association between variables: scatterplots, correlation, and
two-way tables.

### Wednesday — micro-lab ticket

Describe the direction and strength of an association from a graph or
table.

### Friday — quiz / application

Quiz mirroring Wednesday's micro-lab; short headline-critique on
association-vs-causation language.

## Primary source basis

OpenIntro IMS Ch 5 (bivariate numerical) and the intro framing of
Ch 7 (model-slr).

## Supplemental source / custom needs

Headline critique vignettes; light custom material to anchor the
association-vs-causation distinction.

## Learning goals

Students should be able to:

- [ ] Describe direction and strength of association.
- [ ] Interpret correlation cautiously.
- [ ] Avoid causal language for association alone.

## Assessment links

Links to the bank/manifests for this week will appear here once items
are built.

- Micro-lab ticket: TBD
- Friday quiz: TBD
- Homework: TBD
